doc_title: USEPA: FEM: Validation and Peer Review of U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Radiochemical Methods of Analysis

steps.text: Write a detailed procedure and optimize/finalize operational parameters.
Conduct an intralaboratory method validation study.
Clearly describe the components of the method or measurement process requiring validation.
Clearly define the purpose of the method, the intended use of the data, and the method scope and applicability.
Perform sample preservation and storage stability studies during method development.
Investigate the stability of the analyte(s) from the time of sampling through the time of analysis.
Investigate the extract stability if an extraction is performed.
Evaluate method performance characteristics including uncertainty, bias/trueness, precision, quantification capability, detection capability, analyte concentration range, specificity, and ruggedness.
Verify the estimated MDC by testing materials containing analyte at the claimed detection level.
Evaluate method trueness and precision at the extremes of the analyte concentration range.
Evaluate method specificity by testing appropriate matrix blanks and fortified matrix blanks, including a worst-case sample matrix.
Conduct ruggedness testing to identify critical control points.
Conduct an interlaboratory method validation study.
Document any deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol that occur.
Prepare a method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.
Conduct a peer review of the method in accordance with the EPA Science Policy Council's Peer Review Handbook.
Publish the EPA method of analysis.

preconditions.text: Ensure that proper sample preservation and storage stability studies were performed during method development.
A detailed procedure has been written, operational parameters are optimized and finalized, and no additional changes are anticipated.
A successful intralaboratory study has been completed.
Ruggedness testing is completed and critical control points are identified.
An interlaboratory method validation study is completed.
The method is peer reviewed before publication.

constraints.text: If procedural changes are made during an intralaboratory study that may affect performance, repeat some or all performance tests.
If procedural changes are required during an interlaboratory study, repeat some or all aspects of the study.
Address the chemical stability of the molecule if the analysis is based on the molecular form that the radionuclide is in.
Deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol should be strongly discouraged.
